title: manager-prompt-placeholder-leak
description:
This is a continuation of manager-prompt-placeholder-leak. A high-severity prompt placeholder leak was again reported in a manager_watcher prompt at the start of this window, immediately following prior L2 concerns on the same pattern. The current window otherwise appears operationally quiet: manager_watcher, manager_summarizer, and manager_diagnostician all completed successfully, with no failed runs, retry clusters, queue snapshots, or tick activity recorded.

The observed impact in this window is primarily evidence that the factory-internal prompt assembly defect remains unresolved rather than transient. Because the leak is recurring in manager-facing prompts across multiple watcher intervals, it indicates persistent dispatch/prompt plumbing corruption even though it did not cause an execution failure in this slice.

Possible explanations remain limited to factory-side prompt construction or dispatch handling, not story-domain behavior. The unchanged missing-DB worktrees are noted separately by the watcher but show no change in this window and are not the primary concern here.

evidence.summary_excerpt: The previously escalated prompt placeholder leak recurred again in a manager_watcher prompt at the start of this window, and the long-standing set of missing-DB worktrees remains unchanged.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"High-severity placeholder markers recurred in a manager_watcher prompt in this window, continuing the previously escalated prompt-assembly issue."}

escalation_reason: Recurring prompt-placeholder leak in manager_watcher prompts has persisted across multiple watcher-note intervals, including this window ending 2026-05-29T18:49:12.347487+00:00. The pattern is factory-internal and diagnostically clear from the placeholder detector, so L3 judgment is needed to assess the persistent prompt-assembly/dispatch defect even though this slice did not fail.
